title: Launch Academy profile ties together Ph.D., Beckman, and Canada rationale
excerpt: The 2018 Launch Academy / Maple profile is a connective public document linking Sean Xiang's CUHK doctorate, Beckman Laser Institute role, and the strategic case for expanding into Canada.
caption: This establishes a clear connective timeline between his academic period and subsequent geographic expansion.

title: OpenStack community release is the earliest strong public company anchor
excerpt: The February 2013 OpenStack announcement publicly names Sean Xiang as co-founder and CEO while positioning Bloombase around enterprise cloud privacy, compliance, and data-at-rest protection.
caption: This dated release provides early public confirmation of his role and the company's enterprise focus.
visualText: 2013-02-18 · Sean Xiang is quoted as co-founder and CEO while the product is framed as the missing security layer for OpenStack storage.
quoteText: One of the key challenges with enterprise core business applications on cloud adoption is the need to achieve data privacy and regulatory compliance.

mediaItems.title: Co-founder and CEO named in the release
mediaItems.text: The release identifies Sean Xiang directly inside the company narrative, clearly establishing his early leadership.

mediaItems.title: 2013-02-18 opening public anchor
mediaItems.text: Provides an explicit and dated historical claim establishing Bloombase's visibility.

title: The VMware alliance shows Bloombase entering enterprise infrastructure fast
excerpt: The August 2013 VMware Technology Alliance announcement demonstrates Bloombase engaging directly with virtualization and cloud buyers within its first documented year.
caption: A grounded event signaling that Bloombase was built to integrate tightly with trust-heavy infrastructure markets.
visualText: 2013-08-30 · A direct company URL ties Bloombase to VMware's alliance program and to virtualization-era data privacy concerns.
quoteText: Data privacy tops one of CIOs' concerns over virtualization and cloud.

mediaItems.title: Virtualization-era buyer language is present
mediaItems.text: The copy clearly locates Bloombase inside vital virtualization and cloud infrastructure concerns that large enterprise buyers prioritized in 2013.

mediaItems.title: The founding chapter gains market texture
mediaItems.text: Combined with the OpenStack release, the VMware item confirms an infrastructure-facing market trajectory throughout 2013.

title: VMware alliance marks the start of a visible enterprise-validation run
excerpt: Joining VMware's Technology Alliance Partner Program in 2014 serves as a concrete marker of platform interoperability and ecosystem trust.
caption: Establishes a rigorous cadence of recurring validation signals that Bloombase cultivated over the years.

title: VMware Cloud on AWS shows the company translating into the cloud stack
excerpt: The 2018 StoreSafe for VMware Cloud on AWS announcement shows Bloombase advancing its validation story into modern cloud-era deployment surfaces.
caption: Demonstrates continuous evolution alongside shifting global platform paradigms.

title: The NVIDIA DPU / GPU post reframes Bloombase for accelerated computing
excerpt: Bloombase's 2023 positioning—rebuilding data-at-rest security with NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs—signals an effort to translate its security primitives into an AI-driven stack.
caption: This establishes a bridge between historically acquired enterprise credibility and present-tense AI relevance.

mediaItems.title: A technical argument, not a branding refresh
mediaItems.text: This source makes a direct technical argument for overhauling data-at-rest security utilizing NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs.

mediaItems.title: Continuing alignment
mediaItems.text: Positioned securely within high-level AI-centric infrastructure debates and conferences.

title: The Utimaco alliance gives the AI-era chapter a direct primary source
excerpt: The April 2025 Bloombase announcement specifically features the Utimaco alliance supporting data security applications for the AI sector.
caption: A specific and targeted alliance marking further progression into contemporary AI technology.

mediaItems.title: AI-era language is explicit in the release itself
mediaItems.text: Provides an explicit declaration aligning Bloombase's present operations within an AI-oriented landscape.

mediaItems.title: Consistent execution
mediaItems.text: Reflects clear alignment toward industry leadership in contemporary security contexts.

title: Dell certification turns interoperability into dated proof, not generic enterprise name-dropping
excerpt: The January 2014 Dell certification demonstrates Bloombase successfully executing compatibility certifications early within its enterprise validation history.
caption: Highlights an expanding trust profile marked by verifiable platform relationships.
visualText: 2014-01-22 - A dated Bloombase release frames enterprise compatibility as a concrete certification milestone rather than a vague customer-proof claim.

title: The Hitachi alliance shows the validation story widening across storage infrastructure
excerpt: The Hitachi Data Systems alliance demonstrates scaling interoperability across major enterprise-storage environments, widening Bloombase's public integration record.
caption: The company's trust matrix continuously adapts across sequential milestones ranging from basic certification toward complex marketplace validation.
visualText: 2014-11-18 - Bloombase positions itself inside another enterprise storage stack, adding breadth to the validation trail.

title: IBM security validation gives the chapter a stronger mid-period maturity marker
excerpt: The IBM Ready for Security Intelligence milestone functions as an advanced trust signal, showing progression from initial ecosystem entry to sustained enterprise validation.
caption: Provides a maturity marker that further establishes Bloombase evolving intelligently alongside infrastructure markets.
visualText: 2017-03-20 - A direct Bloombase release marks a more mature stage of the company's enterprise-security validation arc.

title: Azure Marketplace proves the validation story reaches public-cloud distribution, not only private infrastructure
excerpt: The 2019 Azure Marketplace listing marks Bloombase successfully expanding its protective operations from strictly local enterprise environments to cloud deployment surfaces.
caption: Shows continuous movement aligning security integration alongside emerging cloud-distribution pipelines.
visualText: 2019-01-22 - Bloombase appears in a public-cloud marketplace, extending the validation arc beyond classic enterprise data-center partnerships.

title: Canada expansion represents a multi-office operational network
excerpt: Establishing physical locations—such as the Vancouver site noted on Bloombase's office page alongside Redwood City, Hong Kong, and Frankfurt—verifies tangible operational expansion beyond Silicon Valley.
caption: Reflects a strategy aligning footprint development with talent accessibility, modern cryptography goals, and ecosystem positioning.
visualText: Redwood City remains the company spine while Vancouver becomes a source-backed expansion chapter connected to talent, cryptography, and ecosystem positioning.

title: Ultra Electronics AEP addresses core key-custody requirements
excerpt: The 2016 Ultra Electronics AEP release expands beyond general platform compatibility by specifically illustrating HSM interoperability, hardened key storage, and foundational cryptographic infrastructure.
caption: Signifies Bloombase's progression into complex, cross-vendor security architectures targeting sensitive key custody environments.
visualText: 2016-06-13 ? Direct Bloombase release ties StoreSafe to hardened HSM-backed key storage and large-scale enterprise deployment concerns.

title: ATTO certification encompasses dense storage-networking requirements
excerpt: The 2017 ATTO certification verifies interoperable operations reaching into highly sensitive physical data paths incorporating Fibre Channel, Ethernet adapters, SAN, and NAS infrastructure.
caption: Ensures complete coverage validating performance across layered hardware requirements.
visualText: 2017-07-17 ? Bloombase frames StoreSafe as interoperable with high-speed storage-networking hardware across SAN and NAS environments.

title: The Slayton advisory-board release expands corporate strategy objectives
excerpt: The October 2014 advisory board announcement signals formalized international operational ambitions backed by experienced strategic guidance.
caption: Illustrates Bloombase transitioning outward, embracing long-range governance and global scaling methodologies.
visualText: 2014-10-08 ? A direct Bloombase release ties Sean Xiang?s company narrative to international growth ambitions and outside strategic counsel.

title: Huawei OceanStor V3 further validates wide-ranging storage interoperability
excerpt: The August 2017 Huawei interoperability release continually expands the trusted security layer touching crucial telecommunications, unified storage networks, and integrated datacenters.
caption: A reinforcement solidifying enterprise infrastructure alignment across consecutive, sequential hardware approvals.
visualText: 2017-08-01 ? Bloombase extends its direct validation trail into Huawei storage infrastructure and telecom / MSP deployment language.
